dataSourceInstructions: Primary data source: Commercial_Spend_Analytics (Direct Lake). Dimensions: dim_date, dim_client, dim_country, dim_merchant, dim_mcc, dim_product, dim_segment, dim_approvalstatus - all join 1:many to fact_commercialspend via their *Key column (TransactionDateKey, ClientKey, CountryKey, MerchantKey, MCCKey, ProductKey, SegmentKey, ApprovalStatusKey). fact_filtersession has NO relationships to any dimension - it stores serialized host-app filter state as strings for grounding only, never join or aggregate it as transactional data. Row-Level Security scopes fact_commercialspend to the caller's dim_client[HomeRegion] via CUSTOMDATA() - assume this is already applied. Use the 9 published measures for all aggregations; never SUM raw fact columns directly (e.g. use [Total Spend USD], not SUM(fact_commercialspend[SpendAmountUSD])).
userDescription: VISA Commercial Spend Analytics Direct Lake semantic model — 250,000 commercial card transactions with 8 dimensions and 9 DAX measures.

elements.description: Grain: one row per synthetic commercial-card transaction. Core fact table for all spend/risk/approval analysis.

children.description: Sum of transaction spend in USD — the program's headline KPI.
Fee revenue earned per transaction — used for savings-opportunity analysis.
0-100 composite risk score; higher = riskier. Not a percentage.
Count of transactions above the fraud threshold.
Current vs. prior calendar year spend growth, using explicit Year filtering (not DATEADD).

elements.description: Grain: one row per host-app filter-state change. Context-grounding only — describes what the user is currently viewing (page, active slicers, selected values). Never aggregate or report on this as spend data; has no relationships to any dimension table.
Calendar dimension; use Year/Quarter for trend and period-comparison questions.

children.description: Client's primary operating region — drives Row-Level Security entitlement via CUSTOMDATA().
Strategic size/importance ranking of the client.
Rolled-up merchant category grouping — used for fraud/decline breakdowns.

elements.description: Client vertical/segment reference (Enterprise, Mid Market, Financial Services, Government, Healthcare, Retail).
